title: Inconsistency In Reported Pod and Container States
body:
### Issue Description

The return value `.State`  for pods and `.State.Status` for containers is inconsistent across pods and containers. 

Pods show "Running" and containers reports "running", noting the case difference.

My searching has not brought any results as to why this may have been done this way.

I am performing some manual health checks and while its probably not the best way to do this, does seem like an odd inconsistency. This is not ruining in a Kube env but in single host env.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start up, run a pod and a single container
2. Perform a ```podman inspect <container> --format "{{.State.Status}}"```
3. Perfrom a ```podman inspect <pod> -type pod --format "{{.State}}"```

You can also perform a manual inspect.

### Describe the results you received

```
[ ~]$ podman inspect <container> --format "{{.State.Status}}" && podman inspect <pod> --type pod --format "{{.State}}"
running
Running
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
[ ~]$ podman inspect <container> --format "{{.State.Status}}" && podman inspect <pod> --type pod --format "{{.State}}"
running
running
```
or "Running" but lowercase seems more consistent with all other values that are retruend

comments.body: We should move the state for Pod to running, since there is a chance people are relying on the container state, less risk of breaking user scripts.

I don't know what is the exact reason but on kubernetes the `pod` status in in caps afaics i.e `Running`, see: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/ and I assume that could have been one of the reason.

Changing `Running` to `running` for kube will mean changing all other events to lower-case as well for consistency, my personal take to keep it as `Running` since it matches with the status of kubernetes. Changing for `kube` is also a breaking change.

In `podman`'s context `pod` and `container` are two different things so I am just wondering if this inconsistency is a big problem or not ( or just a preference )  :smile:
Ok I am fine with that also.

IMO, we should match the casing used for containers. For something larger, we'd have to consider the benefits of matching expected K8S functionality versus doing what a Podman/Docker user not familiar with K8S would expect, but this is just casing; not matching between different parts of Podman is confusing to users and will seem like a bug. We have made no promises about being compatible with `kubectl`, just Kube YAML, so there's no need to worry about our CLI not looking exactly like the output of `kubectl`.

As much as I love consistency, I think it would be a breaking change to `s/Running/running/`.  The fact that we have this issue indicates that users use this information and some may very well depend on it.
Is it worth making this a breaking change in 5.0?
I don´t think this justifies a breaking change since it's something cosmetic.  Some breaking changes are necessary but the fewer the better IMO.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
